steps.title: Rewrite the radical
steps.content:
Use the rule that a root can be written as a fractional exponent:
$$
\sqrt[14]{(315n)^9}=(315n)^{\frac{9}{14}}
$$

steps.title: Match the exponents
steps.content:
Both expressions have the same positive base, so set the exponents equal:
$$
\frac{9}{14}=24x
$$

steps.title: Isolate the variable
steps.content: Divide by $24$: $$ x=\frac{9}{14}\cdot\frac{1}{24}=\frac{9}{336} $$ Reducing the fraction gives the final value. The answer is 3/112.
